title: phaseolus sordidus
intro: Phaseolus sordidus, also known as the Indian butter bean or moth bean, is a type of legume native to the Indian subcontinent. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antidiabetic effects. The health benefits of Phaseolus sordidus include reducing blood sugar levels, improving heart health, and aiding in weight management. Herbal preparations of Phaseolus sordidus can be made into decoctions, infusions, or powders, which are often used to treat various health conditions, including diabetes, obesity, and cardiovascular diseases.
